subtitle: A Gupta Princess Who Ruled the Deccan

panels.shot: majestic low-angle mid-shot framed by Deccan hills and a distant plateau fortress
panels.action: Prabhāvatīgupta stands in regal stillness on a stone terrace, one hand resting on a rolled copper-plate grant, the other on the railing, as the hazy Deccan plain stretches beneath a monsoon sky.

dialogue.text: Prabhāvatīgupta, daughter of the Guptas and regent of the Vākāṭakas, holds the Deccan steady between north and south.

i18n.hi: गुप्तकुल की पुत्री प्रभावतीगुप्ता, वाकाटक राज्य की राजप्रतिनिधि, दक्कन को उत्तर‑दक्षिण के बीच थामे है।

panels.shot: broad establishing aerial of the subcontinent with subtle emphasis on north and Deccan
panels.action: A stylized map-like vista shows the Ganga plain with imperial cities in the north and the rugged Deccan plateau in the south, rivers glinting like silver threads between them under a soft dawn light.

dialogue.text: In the fourth century of the Common Era, the Guptas rise in the north, while the Vākāṭakas guard the Deccan heights.

i18n.hi: ईसा की चौथी शताब्दी में उत्तर में गुप्त उठते हैं, और दक्कन की पहाड़ियों पर वाकाटक पहरा देते हैं।

panels.shot: intimate interior mid-shot of a royal girl in study
panels.action: Young Prabhāvatīgupta sits cross-legged in a quiet palace chamber, lit by oil lamps, listening intently to an unseen teacher as palm-leaf manuscripts lie open before her.

dialogue.text: Born to Chandragupta II’s house, Prabhāvatīgupta grows amid poets, priests, and policy, learning how empires breathe.

i18n.hi: चंद्रगुप्त द्वितीय के घर जन्मी प्रभावतीगुप्ता कवियों, पुरोहितों और राजनीति के बीच पलती है, साम्राज्य की सांस समझती हुई।

panels.shot: ceremonial wide-shot of a marriage alliance scene
panels.action: In a grand pavilion adorned with garlands, Prabhāvatīgupta walks towards an unseen groom, veiled but steady, while behind her stand courtiers in mixed northern and Deccan attire, watching with measured hope.

dialogue.text: She is given in marriage to Rudrasena II of the Vākāṭakas, a living bond tying Gupta north to Deccan south.

i18n.hi: उसे वाकाटक नरेश रुद्रसेन द्वितीय से ब्याह दिया जाता है, जो गुप्त उत्तर को दक्कन दक्षिण से बाँधने वाली जीवित कड़ी है।

panels.shot: mid-shot on palace balcony overlooking Deccan landscape
panels.action: Prabhāvatīgupta stands beside the crenellations of a Vākāṭaka fort, gazing over red earth fields and basalt ridges, her jewelry now in the style of the Deccan, her posture already queenly.

dialogue.text: In Vidarbha’s forts and fields she learns a new land—the accents, the soil, the fears that ride on its winds.

i18n.hi: विदर्भ के किलों और खेतों में वह नई भूमि सीखती है—नई बोली, नई मिट्टी, और हवाओं पर सवार डर।

panels.shot: somber interior wide-shot of royal court with empty throne
panels.action: In the main audience hall, a great throne stands empty in shadow; Prabhāvatīgupta is seated slightly below it, draped in white, as courtiers bow with anxious faces and messengers kneel with sealed scrolls.

dialogue.text: Rudrasena II dies young. The throne of the Vākāṭakas stands bare, while his sons are still children.

i18n.hi: रुद्रसेन द्वितीय कम उम्र में ही मर जाता है। वाकाटक सिंहासन सूना रह जाता है, उसके बेटे अभी बच्चे हैं।

panels.shot: low-angle close mid-shot from behind the council looking up at her
panels.action: Prabhāvatīgupta sits upright on a slightly raised seat before the council, eyes calm yet firm, one hand resting protectively on a small prince’s shoulder at her side.

dialogue.text: In that hollow of power, the widowed queen steps forward as regent, ruling in her sons’ name and in her own.

i18n.hi: उस खाली सत्ता में विधवा रानी आगे बढ़ती है, राजप्रतिनिधि बनकर, बेटों के नाम पर और अपने नाम से राज करती है।

panels.shot: close-up on her hands holding a copper-plate grant
panels.action: Her hands, adorned with modest bangles, hold a freshly inscribed copper plate; the engraver kneels nearby, tools laid out, awaiting her final nod.

dialogue.text: Copper plates from Poona and Riddhapur will later tell her story, for they bear her orders, not another’s.

i18n.hi: पूना और ऋद्धापुर की ताम्रपट बाद में उसकी कथा कहेंगे, क्योंकि उन पर उसकी ही आज्ञाएँ खुदी हैं, किसी और की नहीं।

panels.shot: thoughtful profile close-up as she listens to petitioners
panels.action: In a shaded audience, Prabhāvatīgupta listens as village headmen gesture towards unseen lands, her profile lit by slanting light, brows slightly furrowed in concentration.

dialogue.text: She grants land to Brāhmaṇas and institutions, binding frontier villages into the king’s law with plate and seal.

i18n.hi: वह ब्राह्मणों और संस्थाओं को भूमि देती है, सीमा के गाँवों को ताम्रपत्र और मुहर से राजधर्म में बाँधती है।

panels.shot: rear three-quarter shot of her overseeing scribes at a long table
panels.action: Prabhāvatīgupta stands behind a low table where scribes crouch, incising scripts onto plates; she points to a passage, gently but decisively correcting a line as they glance up in respect.

dialogue.text: Write this clearly: these lands are granted under my regency.

i18n.hi: इसे साफ लिखो: ये भूमि मेरी राजप्रतिनिधि के नाम पर दी जाती है।

panels.shot: medium shot, slightly low angle, as she addresses skeptical nobles
panels.action: In the hall, a knot of armored nobles stands with stiff shoulders; Prabhāvatīgupta faces them, chin raised, gesturing with an open palm toward the throne behind her.

dialogue.text: Some bristle at a woman’s hand upon the realm, yet the copper plates call her mahādevī, born of the Gupta gotra.

i18n.hi: कुछ लोगों को राज्य पर स्त्री का हाथ खटकता है, पर ताम्रपट उसे महादेवी कहते हैं, गुप्त गोत्रज।

panels.shot: interior close-up of an engraved line on a plate, her reflection faint on the metal
panels.action: The focus rests on the incised phrase declaring her lineage and authority, while a faint reflection of Prabhāvatīgupta’s face shimmers on the polished surface.

dialogue.text: She names herself of the Dhāraṇa Gupta line, letting all know the northern empire stands behind her rule.

i18n.hi: वह खुद को धारणा गुप्त कुल की बताती है, ताकि सब जानें कि उत्तरी साम्राज्य उसकी पीठ पर खड़ा है।

panels.shot: wide rural shot of her chariot arriving in a village
panels.action: Her modest yet stately chariot halts at the edge of a Deccan village; villagers, priests, and local chiefs bow as she steps down, feet touching the dusty track, looking around with quiet assessment.

dialogue.text: Year after year, she steadies outlying lands, hearing disputes, remitting dues, weighing justice in unfamiliar dialects.

i18n.hi: साल दर साल वह दूर दराज की भूमि सँभालती है, झगड़े सुनती है, लगान घटाती‑बढ़ाती है, अनजानी बोलियों में न्याय तौलती है।

panels.shot: montage wide-shot of artisans and monks near rocky cliffs
panels.action: At the base of a rocky escarpment, artisans chip at stone faces while monks discuss plans, tracing lines on the cliff, the scene suggesting the early stirrings of future cave sanctuaries.

dialogue.text: Under the Vākāṭakas, the Deccan’s wealth will one day carve sanctuaries like Ajanta into living rock.

i18n.hi: वाकाटकों के अधीन दक्कन की दौलत एक दिन अजंता जैसे मंदिरों को चट्टानों में तराशेगी।

panels.shot: quiet interior mid-shot of her at a shrine
panels.action: In a small palace shrine, Prabhāvatīgupta places a garland on a linga, lamps flickering around her, while outside the faint echo of courtly bustle drifts in.

dialogue.text: Her grants favor Śaiva shrines and Brāhmaṇa settlements, weaving devotion, learning, and land into one fabric.

i18n.hi: उसकी दानपट्टियाँ शैव मंदिरों और ब्राह्मण बसाहटों को बढ़ावा देती हैं, भक्ति, विद्या और भूमि को एक धागे में पिरोती हुई।

panels.shot: gentle interior wide-shot of the maturing princes with her in the background
panels.action: Two young princes practice archery in a courtyard, supervised by tutors; in the colonnade behind, Prabhāvatīgupta watches from a carved pillar’s shadow, hands folded, eyes both proud and distant.

dialogue.text: Her sons grow into their inheritance while she keeps the throne warm, never letting the kingdom see a crack.

i18n.hi: उसके बेटे धीरे‑धीरे अपना हक सँभालते हैं, और वह सिंहासन को गरम रखती है, राज्य में दरार पड़ने नहीं देती।

panels.shot: transition wide-shot of court as she steps back from the central seat
panels.action: In the audience hall, a prince now sits on the main throne; Prabhāvatīgupta stands slightly to the side, head inclined, as if passing an invisible weight from her shoulders to his.

dialogue.text: Around the middle of the fifth century, the regency fades, but the order she set in place endures.

i18n.hi: पाँचवीं शताब्दी के बीच आते‑आते राजप्रतिनिधि की छाया धुंधली पड़ती है, पर उसका बसाया हुआ नियम कायम रहता है।

panels.shot: close-up on her aging face looking at a stored copper plate
panels.action: An older Prabhāvatīgupta sits in a quieter chamber, fingertips resting on a box of rolled plates, her face lined yet serene as she looks towards an unseen horizon.

dialogue.text: Let them remember that a woman’s hand once signed these lands.

i18n.hi: याद रखें, कभी एक स्त्री के हाथ ने भी इन ज़मीनों पर दस्तखत किए थे।

panels.shot: timeless wide-shot blending her figure with a backdrop of Ajanta-like caves and a map
panels.action: Her stately silhouette stands in the foreground, while behind her the outline of a map connects the Ganga plain to the Deccan, and afar, sculpted cave facades loom, bathed in soft golden light.

dialogue.text: Prabhāvatīgupta’s reign of ink and will binds Gupta and Vākāṭaka, proving that wisdom at the center can hold a realm as firmly as any sword.

i18n.hi: स्याही और इरादे के उसकी हुकूमत गुप्त और वाकाटक को बाँध देती है, दिखाती है कि बीच की बुद्धि भी राज्य को उतना ही मज़बूत थाम सकती है जितना तलवार।

subtitle_i18n.en: A Gupta Princess Who Ruled the Deccan
subtitle_i18n.hi: एक गुप्त राजकुमारी जिसने दक्कन संभाला
